name: Phil Bodner
abstract: Philip L. Bodner (June 13, 1917 – February 24, 2008) was an American jazz clarinetist and studio musician who also played flute, oboe, saxophone, and English horn.

concerts.label: JVC Jazz Festival: For the Love of Louis
JVC Jazz Festival: Mel Tormé and Shirley Horn, Singers

works.t: Lew Leslie's Blackbirds of 1930: Memories of You
Can-Can: It’s All Right with Me

about_quotes.speaker: Lou Marini
about_quotes.evidence: But guys like Romeo Penque and Phil Bodner who were on those classic Gil Evans albums © Fillius Jazz Archive -10- with Miles were my colleagues. I overlapped them in the studios.
about_quotes.doc_title: Lou Marini interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 15, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Ray Shiner
about_quotes.evidence: And the three of us are looking at each other... Phil was the diplomat among the three of us... So we played the same notes
about_quotes.doc_title: Ray Shiner interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, October 11, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Dick Hyman
about_quotes.evidence: I’m talking about people like Phil Bodner say, the reed player... And my colleagues in the piano department were...
about_quotes.doc_title: Dick Hyman interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 17, 2001 [interview 2 of 2]
